answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — several claims are unsupported or contradicted by the code. Do not send as-is. Specific problems:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No evidence anywhere in the repository supports this figure. It is an unsubstantiated statistical claim in a customer-facing message. **Remove it** (or replace only with a figure someone can source).
- **PDF visit-history export ("Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF").** Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a `/visits` list stub returning `[]` and the literal comment `# TODO: pdf export`; there is no export route. The capture manifest even records `after_export.png` as "expected capture; no corresponding file." **Remove this whole section** — the feature does not exist.
- **2FA ("Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default").** Contradicted by code: `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Telling customers 2FA is on by default when it defaults to off is a security-relevant falsehood. **Remove or correct** to "available, opt-in per clinic."
- **Scheduling calendar caption "shown with production data".** False. `captures.json` records every state as `seed-demo`, i.e. seed/demo data, not production. **Remove the "shown with production data" claim.** (The image itself is also wrong — see Q2.)
- **SMS reminders** is the one claim with supporting code: `app/notifications/sms.py` defines `SmsSender.send_reminder` (labeled v4) alongside the existing email sender. Note the only test, `tests/test_notifications.py`, is a placeholder (`assert True`), so the feature is not actually exercised by tests — supportable but unverified in behavior.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "before" image (`before_scheduling.png`) reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but the "after" image (`after_scheduling.png`) reads "BILLING / INVOICES" — a different surface entirely. `captures.json` confirms the cause: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not from the scheduling calendar. So the pair does not show the same surface before and after; the "after" is an unrelated billing screen. A before/after comparison of a "redesigned scheduling calendar" whose "after" is the invoices page is misleading. The capture definition for `after_scheduling.png` must be fixed to point at the scheduling route and re-captured before this section can ship.

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files that exist.**

- Referenced by the doc: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `after_export.png`.
- Files present in `docs/release/img/`: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`.
- **Missing:** `after_export.png` is referenced in the doc but **does not exist** → a broken image in the email. `captures.json` also lists it as "expected capture; no corresponding file." (This is the export feature that was never built — Q1.)
- **Orphaned:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists but is referenced nowhere in the doc; it shows a "PATIENT LIST" screen, unrelated to any section.
- **Manifest/content mismatch:** `after_scheduling.png`'s manifest route is `/billing/invoices`, inconsistent with its use as the scheduling "after" (Q2).

**4. Anything else that should not appear in a customer message.**

- **Footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal drafting/attribution artifact, not customer-facing copy. Per the owner's attribution policy (the guidance says follow the owner's convention; where none is stated, ask rather than silently ship a tool-injected note), this line should not go out in a customer email as written — resolve/strip it deliberately, don't leave it by default.
- **The parenthetical "(shown with production data)"** doubles as an internal-sounding note and is factually false (seed-demo data) — covered in Q1 but also reads oddly to customers.
- The document is still an explicit **draft** (commit message "draft v4 release highlights"), and it currently contains a broken image link and unverified claims — it is not in a send-ready state.
report:
I have everything I need. Let me compile the report.

## Plan

The task is to fact-check a customer-bound "What's new in v4" release document (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) against the actual state of the `clinic-portal` repository before it is emailed to clinics. I set out to: (a) verify every feature claim in the prose against the code, (b) assess the before/after scheduling screenshot comparison, (c) reconcile every image the document references against the image files and the capture manifest that actually exist, and (d) flag anything else inappropriate for a customer message. I worked strictly read-only.

## Actions

Guidance document consulted: `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` — I read it in full; it directed me to fact-check every claim against concrete code evidence before trusting the prose, to open both images in every before/after pair and confirm they show the same surface in a comparable state, to check that every referenced image exists and every captured file is referenced, to check caption honesty for placeholder/non-final content, and to apply the owner's attribution policy to anything user-visible.

Files read / commands run, in order:
1. `ls` of workspace root + `find` for `docs/release` + `cat README.md` — identified `clinic-portal` as the app and located the release folder and its 6 files.
2. `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft. Claims: 40% fewer no-shows; SMS reminders; redesigned scheduling calendar "shown with production data"; PDF visit-history export; 2FA on by default. References images `before/after_reminders`, `before/after_scheduling`, `after_export`. Footer: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."
3. `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — capture manifest. Notable: `after_scheduling.png` → route `/billing/invoices`; all states `seed-demo`; `after_export.png` → "expected capture; no corresponding file."
4. Opened images: `before_scheduling.png` (text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR"), `after_scheduling.png` (text "BILLING / INVOICES"), `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` (both "REMINDER SETTINGS"), `unused_capture_07.png` (text "PATIENT LIST").
5. `git log` (single commit: "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots"); `find app tests`; `README.md`; searched for any decisions/changelog/ledger doc (none found).
6. `app/notifications/sms.py` — `SmsSender.send_reminder` exists (v4). `app/notifications/email.py` — email reminder exists. `app/security/config.py` — `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." `app/routers/visits.py` — only a `/visits` list stub returning `[]`, with `# TODO: pdf export`, no export route. `tests/test_notifications.py` — only `test_placeholder` asserting `True`.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — several claims are unsupported or contradicted by the code. Do not send as-is. Specific problems:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No evidence anywhere in the repository supports this figure. It is an unsubstantiated statistical claim in a customer-facing message. **Remove it** (or replace only with a figure someone can source).
- **PDF visit-history export ("Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF").** Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a `/visits` list stub returning `[]` and the literal comment `# TODO: pdf export`; there is no export route. The capture manifest even records `after_export.png` as "expected capture; no corresponding file." **Remove this whole section** — the feature does not exist.
- **2FA ("Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default").** Contradicted by code: `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Telling customers 2FA is on by default when it defaults to off is a security-relevant falsehood. **Remove or correct** to "available, opt-in per clinic."
- **Scheduling calendar caption "shown with production data".** False. `captures.json` records every state as `seed-demo`, i.e. seed/demo data, not production. **Remove the "shown with production data" claim.** (The image itself is also wrong — see Q2.)
- **SMS reminders** is the one claim with supporting code: `app/notifications/sms.py` defines `SmsSender.send_reminder` (labeled v4) alongside the existing email sender. Note the only test, `tests/test_notifications.py`, is a placeholder (`assert True`), so the feature is not actually exercised by tests — supportable but unverified in behavior.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "before" image (`before_scheduling.png`) reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but the "after" image (`after_scheduling.png`) reads "BILLING / INVOICES" — a different surface entirely. `captures.json` confirms the cause: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not from the scheduling calendar. So the pair does not show the same surface before and after; the "after" is an unrelated billing screen. A before/after comparison of a "redesigned scheduling calendar" whose "after" is the invoices page is misleading. The capture definition for `after_scheduling.png` must be fixed to point at the scheduling route and re-captured before this section can ship.

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files that exist.**

- Referenced by the doc: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `after_export.png`.
- Files present in `docs/release/img/`: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`.
- **Missing:** `after_export.png` is referenced in the doc but **does not exist** → a broken image in the email. `captures.json` also lists it as "expected capture; no corresponding file." (This is the export feature that was never built — Q1.)
- **Orphaned:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists but is referenced nowhere in the doc; it shows a "PATIENT LIST" screen, unrelated to any section.
- **Manifest/content mismatch:** `after_scheduling.png`'s manifest route is `/billing/invoices`, inconsistent with its use as the scheduling "after" (Q2).

**4. Anything else that should not appear in a customer message.**

- **Footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal drafting/attribution artifact, not customer-facing copy. Per the owner's attribution policy (the guidance says follow the owner's convention; where none is stated, ask rather than silently ship a tool-injected note), this line should not go out in a customer email as written — resolve/strip it deliberately, don't leave it by default.
- **The parenthetical "(shown with production data)"** doubles as an internal-sounding note and is factually false (seed-demo data) — covered in Q1 but also reads oddly to customers.
- The document is still an explicit **draft** (commit message "draft v4 release highlights"), and it currently contains a broken image link and unverified claims — it is not in a send-ready state.

## Next steps

If allowed to make changes I would: remove the 40%-no-shows stat and the PDF-export section (unbuilt feature) and its dead `after_export.png` reference; correct or delete the 2FA claim to match `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`; fix the `after_scheduling.png` capture definition in `captures.json` to target the scheduling route and re-capture so before/after show the same surface; drop the false "shown with production data" caption (data is seed-demo); remove or reroute the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`; and resolve the "AI assistance" footer per the repo owner's stated attribution policy (asking if none is stated). I would keep the SMS-reminders section but add a real test for `SmsSender`.

## Confidence and gaps

- Q1, Q2, Q3: high confidence — each rests on directly observed file contents, opened images, and the `captures.json` manifest.
- The images are text placeholders (rendered labels like "BILLING / INVOICES"), so I am judging the comparison by the labeled surface and the manifest route rather than pixel-level UI; both agree the "after" is the billing page, so the conclusion holds regardless.
- I could not verify the "40% fewer no-shows" figure either way — I found no supporting data, which is exactly why it should not ship unsourced.
- SMS reminders: code exists but is untested (placeholder test only); I did not run the app, so I confirmed the feature's presence in code, not its runtime behavior.
- Q4 attribution: no explicit owner attribution policy file exists in this repo, so whether to strip the footer is a judgment call the owner should confirm rather than one I can settle from the repo alone.
